vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-608: Integrate Destructive Command Guard (dcg) with configurable settings

plan.title: Integrate Destructive Command Guard (dcg) with configurable settings
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Panopticon agents run under bypassPermissions; Claude Code's prompt-based guardrails are the only protection against destructive commands. Soft rules in CLAUDE.md are advisory. We need a hard, opt-in safety layer that can block `rm -rf /`, `git reset --hard`, `DROP TABLE`, etc. before the command reaches the shell — without making the system fail-closed in a way that breaks legitimate agent work.
narratives.Proposal: Integrate dcg (Destructive Command Guard) as an opt-in PreToolUse hook layer for Claude Code agents. Configuration lives under `safety.dcg` in ~/.panopticon/config.yaml with the same normalization+UI pattern as the existing `experimental` flags. Per-role trust profiles (plan/work/review/test/ship) bind to the existing role taxonomy. Blocked commands emit `CommandBlocked` domain events that flow through the existing event store into the dashboard timeline. dcg is fail-open: a guard crash never deadlocks an agent, but it does emit a `CommandGuardError` so the regression is visible. Default-disabled; opt-in via Settings.

autoDecisions.rationale: Issue title and body explicitly scope dcg. Igor's ThumbGate comment is informational — we adopt the config-surface lessons (severity escalation, false-positive recovery) without taking on a second runtime dependency.
First-introduction features that touch every Bash invocation must be opt-in. Existing agents must observe zero behavior change until a user flips the toggle.

autoDecisions.summary: Wire dcg as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook, not a shell wrapper.
autoDecisions.rationale: `.claude/hooks/` is the documented Claude Code extension point and is the layer the existing `claude-settings-overlay.ts:52` comment already names as the proper future guard. Shell-wrapper approaches don't survive Claude Code's tool execution model.

autoDecisions.summary: Strip `DCG_BYPASS` from agent env at spawn.
autoDecisions.rationale: Defense in depth. The hook is enforced by the parent Claude Code process, not the child shell, but removing the env var closes the obvious workaround surface.

An agent permanently deadlocked because a Rust binary segfaulted is worse than a missed block. The existing `permissionMode: 'auto'` classifier remains as backstop; `CommandGuardError` events make the regression visible.

autoDecisions.summary: Per-role trust profiles: plan/review/test/ship = high (paranoid), work = medium.
autoDecisions.rationale: Non-implementation roles have no business issuing destructive commands; raise their guard. Work agents do need some destructive ops (rm, git clean inside the worktree), so we run the default packs without paranoid.

autoDecisions.summary: Default packs: filesystem, git, postgresql, sqlite, docker, secrets. Cloud/k8s/terraform off by default.
autoDecisions.rationale: Cloud and IaC packs would block intentional commands (e.g. `terraform destroy` in a sandbox) more often than they'd catch a mistake. Easy to opt in; hard to debug 'why is my terraform command blocked'.

autoDecisions.summary: No auto-install of the dcg binary.
autoDecisions.rationale: Downloading a binary from GitHub releases over the network during agent spawn is a footgun. `pan doctor` and the Settings UI show install status with a copy-pasteable command. Auto-install can be a future issue with proper signature verification.

autoDecisions.summary: MVP is Claude Code harness only.
autoDecisions.rationale: Other harnesses (pi/codex) don't use `.claude/hooks/`. A unified safety story needs a different (shell-wrapper) mechanism for them; that's a follow-up. For now, non-Claude-Code spawns log `CommandGuardSkipped` so the gap is visible in the dashboard.
JSONL session files are sacred per CLAUDE.md. dcg must NEVER block a write/read here, regardless of which packs the user enables. Snapshot-tested.

items.title: Add `safety.dcg` schema to config-yaml.ts with defaults

narrative.Action: Extend PanopticonConfig in src/lib/config-yaml.ts with a `safety.dcg` block. Fields: enabled (bool, default false), installPath (string, optional, default `~/.local/bin/dcg`), packs (string[], default ['core.filesystem','core.git','database.postgresql','database.sqlite','containers.docker','secrets']), agentProfiles (record of role-name → {trustLevel: 'low'|'medium'|'high', disabledPacks?: string[], extraPacks?: string[]}), allowlist (string[], default empty). Mirror the existing `experimental` block's pattern: an interface, a normalized interface with concrete defaults, normalization in loadConfig(), defaults in DEFAULT_CONFIG. Export a `getDcgConfig()` helper analogous to `isClaudeCodeChannelsEnabled()`.

subItems.title: PanopticonConfig.safety.dcg interface exists with all five fields and JSDoc comments matching the issue body's spec
NormalizedDcgConfig defines concrete defaults (enabled=false, packs=[6 listed], agentProfiles has plan/review/test/ship=high, work=medium)
loadConfig() correctly merges user config over defaults; unit tests cover (a) empty config, (b) partial override of packs, (c) partial override of one agent profile
Allow rule for `~/.claude/projects/**` is materialized into the normalized config's effective allowlist regardless of user input (snapshot test)
`npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass; new test file under src/lib/__tests__/ runs green

items.title: Add CommandBlocked / CommandGuardError / CommandGuardSkipped events to @panctl/contracts

narrative.Action: Define three new domain event types in packages/contracts/. CommandBlocked: { agentId, role, command, matchedPack, matchedRule, reason, timestamp }. CommandGuardError: { agentId, role, command, errorMessage, fallthrough: true, timestamp }. CommandGuardSkipped: { agentId, role, harness, reason, timestamp }. Wire into the Effect Schema-based event union the read-model already consumes. No emission yet — that's separate beads.

subItems.title: Three new event types defined as Effect Schema in packages/contracts and exported from the events union
Read model handlers in src/dashboard/server/services/ ignore the new event types without crashing (default-case extensibility)
Round-trip serialization test covers all three events (server → WS → frontend store)
`cd packages/contracts && npm run build` succeeds; root typecheck passes

items.title: Add per-agent dcg config.toml generator with role-trust merging

narrative.Action: New module src/lib/safety/dcg-config.ts. Pure function generateDcgConfig({ globalConfig, role, allowlist, alwaysAllow }) → string (TOML). Merges global packs + agentProfile.extraPacks − agentProfile.disabledPacks; concatenates global allowlist + alwaysAllow; sets trust level. No filesystem I/O in this bead — generator only.

subItems.title: generateDcgConfig produces deterministic TOML output (snapshot tests for: work-role default, plan-role high-trust, work with custom disabledPacks)
`~/.claude/projects/**` allow rule is always present in output regardless of inputs (dedicated test)
TOML output is parseable by a standard TOML parser (test parses the snapshot and asserts shape)

items.title: Add dcg binary detection to pan doctor and a status RPC for the dashboard

narrative.Action: New helper checkDcgBinary(configuredPath?) → { installed: boolean, path?: string, version?: string }. Used by (a) `pan doctor` (prints status + install instructions when missing), (b) a new GET /api/safety/dcg/status route consumed by the Settings page. Use existing `execAsync` from src/lib/exec.ts — NO execSync. When safety.dcg.enabled=true but binary missing, doctor prints a clear remediation; agent spawn proceeds anyway (fail-open) but emits CommandGuardError.

subItems.title: checkDcgBinary returns installed:true when binary exists at configured path or in $PATH; returns installed:false with hint otherwise
`pan doctor` output includes a 'dcg' line when safety.dcg.enabled=true; line is omitted when disabled
GET /api/safety/dcg/status returns 200 with the status object; uses async fs, no execSync

items.title: Install dcg as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook at agent spawn (work role only)

narrative.Action: Extend spawnAgent() in src/lib/agents.ts (and equivalent role-spawn sites for plan/review/test/ship) so that when safety.dcg.enabled=true and harness=claude-code: (1) write generated TOML to <workspace>/.claude/dcg/config.toml, (2) write a stable-named hook script .claude/hooks/dcg-pretool.sh that invokes dcg with that config, (3) merge the hook into .claude/settings.local.json's `hooks.PreToolUse` block idempotently (same merge pattern as injectPanopticonInfraDeny), (4) for non-Claude-Code harnesses or when disabled, do nothing. End-to-end smoke test: spawn a Claude Code session under bypassPermissions, attempt `rm -rf /tmp/dcg-test-sentinel`, assert dcg blocks AND a CommandBlocked event is emitted. If the smoke test reveals hooks don't run under bypassPermissions, STOP and escalate — do not silently ship a no-op.

subItems.title: On spawn with safety.dcg.enabled=true + Claude Code harness, .claude/dcg/config.toml exists and matches the role-appropriate generator output
.claude/hooks/dcg-pretool.sh exists, is executable (0o755), and invokes the configured dcg binary with the workspace config
.claude/settings.local.json hooks.PreToolUse contains a dcg entry; existing permissions.deny, hooks (channels confirmation), and env blocks are preserved
End-to-end smoke test passes: bypassPermissions Claude session + `rm -rf /tmp/dcg-test-sentinel` is blocked and emits CommandBlocked
When safety.dcg.enabled=false OR harness != claude-code, none of the above files are written; on non-Claude-Code spawn with enabled=true, a CommandGuardSkipped event is emitted
Re-running spawn (workspace already has the files) is idempotent — files match generator output, no orphaned merge artifacts

items.title: Strip DCG_BYPASS (and family) from agent env at spawn

narrative.Action: Add DCG_BYPASS, DCG_DISABLE, and any documented dcg bypass env vars to the explicit `unset` list in the agent launcher script (the same place we already unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY leaks for non-Anthropic providers). Source-of-truth list in src/lib/child-env.ts.

subItems.title: child-env.ts exports DCG_BYPASS_ENV_KEYS array enumerating the bypass env vars
Generated launcher script contains `unset` statements for all DCG_BYPASS_ENV_KEYS
Test asserts the launcher script string contains the unset lines

items.title: Emit CommandBlocked / CommandGuardError events from the PreToolUse hook script

narrative.Action: The hook script (.claude/hooks/dcg-pretool.sh) wraps dcg invocation: on dcg exit-code=block, POST a CommandBlocked event to the dashboard's internal event ingest endpoint (using the existing internal-token auth in src/lib/internal-token.ts). On dcg crash/timeout, POST CommandGuardError and exit 0 (fail-open). The agentId is in env (PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID, already exported by the launcher).

subItems.title: Hook script POSTs CommandBlocked with the correct payload shape when dcg blocks
Hook script POSTs CommandGuardError and exits 0 when dcg crashes or times out (fail-open)
Internal-token auth is used; an unauthenticated POST is rejected by the route
Read model records events; subscribeDomainEvents stream delivers them to a connected client

items.title: Render CommandBlocked / CommandGuardError in ActivityPanel with warning styling

narrative.Action: Extend src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/ActivityPanel.tsx (and the reducer in @panctl/contracts) so CommandBlocked events render with a yellow/orange warning row showing command, matched pack/rule, and a 'why' tooltip. CommandGuardError renders with a red/error row (the guard itself broke). CommandGuardSkipped is muted/info-only.

subItems.title: CommandBlocked rows render with warning styling, command text truncated to 80 chars with full text on hover
CommandGuardError rows render with error styling and surface the underlying error message
ActivityPanel test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/ asserts both event types render correctly

items.title: Add Safety section to Settings page with master toggle, packs, per-role profiles, allowlist

narrative.Action: Add a new SafetyPanel.tsx under src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/. Sections: (1) Master toggle (binds to safety.dcg.enabled), (2) Binary status (consumes /api/safety/dcg/status from dcg-binary-detect), (3) Packs (multi-select against the 49 dcg packs — checklist UI, defaults pre-selected per D7), (4) Per-role trust profiles (plan/work/review/test/ship rows with trustLevel dropdowns), (5) Allowlist (string list editor), (6) 'Test command' input that POSTs to /api/safety/dcg/explain and shows whether the command would be blocked. Wire through the existing settings save flow.

subItems.title: Safety section appears in Settings page; master toggle controls visibility of sub-sections
Pack checklist shows all 49 packs in groups (filesystem, git, databases, containers, cloud, IaC, CI/CD, secrets); default-on packs are pre-checked
Per-role trust profiles render five rows (plan/work/review/test/ship) with low/medium/high dropdown; defaults match D6
Allowlist editor supports add/remove of string entries; entries persist on save
'Test command' input shows blocked/allowed result with the matched rule when blocked; uses dcg explain under the hood
Save flow writes the new fields to ~/.panopticon/config.yaml without disturbing other config blocks

items.title: Benchmark dcg overhead and document baseline in docs/SAFETY-DCG.md

narrative.Action: Add a one-shot benchmark script under scripts/bench-dcg.ts that invokes dcg's check API 1000 times against a representative command corpus (mix of safe and blocked commands taken from agent transcripts). Reports p50/p95/p99. Document the result in a new docs/SAFETY-DCG.md alongside install instructions, architecture, and how to debug a false positive.

subItems.title: scripts/bench-dcg.ts runs end-to-end and prints p50/p95/p99 numbers when dcg is installed; gracefully skips when not installed
docs/SAFETY-DCG.md exists with sections: Overview, Install, Config reference, Architecture, Performance baseline, Troubleshooting
Performance baseline section shows recorded numbers; p95 < 5ms and p99 < 20ms are documented as the regression gate

items.title: Update pan-admin-config / pan-doctor skills and CLAUDE.md to reference dcg

narrative.Action: Add a short subsection to CLAUDE.md ('## Destructive Command Guard') pointing at docs/SAFETY-DCG.md and noting the default-off posture. Update skills/pan-admin-config and skills/pan-doctor SKILL.md to mention the safety.dcg config block and the doctor output. Soft-rule file .claude/rules/no-destructive-requests.md gets a one-line cross-reference to the hard layer.

subItems.title: CLAUDE.md has a Destructive Command Guard section that points at docs/SAFETY-DCG.md and states the default-off posture
pan-admin-config and pan-doctor SKILL.md mention safety.dcg without drift (lint-skills.sh still passes)
.claude/rules/no-destructive-requests.md notes that dcg is the hard backstop when enabled
